bundle_identity.name: MCP Agent Tooling Prompt Bundle
bundle_identity.description: Canonical ResearchOps bundle for Model Context Protocol, tool contracts, resource exposure, prompt workflows, consent checkpoints, client features, utilities, authorization and agent-tooling safety.

policy_interpretation.rule: require_explicit_tool_consent means tool invocation is gated; it does not mean tools are disabled.
Tools and resources should remain discoverable to the user and agent unless unavailable for transport, configuration or runtime reasons.
When consent or permission is missing, return a blocked status and remediation instructions instead of reporting tools as disabled.
Use tool_unavailable only for genuine availability faults, not for consent or permission gating outcomes.

tier.name: auto-allow-read-only
tier.definition: Read-only, low-risk operations may be invoked without per-call consent when they do not change state, expose sensitive data or create chargeable side effects.

tier.name: explicit-invocation-consent-required
tier.definition: Write or mutate actions, sensitive-data access and cost-bearing operations require explicit user consent before invocation.

tier.name: elevated-approval-required
tier.definition: High-impact, irreversible, privilege-changing or broad-scope operations require elevated approval in addition to standard invocation consent.

recommended_invocation_policy.rule: Keep tools discoverable by default and gate invocation using trust tiers and consent state.
Allow tier-1 operations under least privilege with audit logging.
Gate tier-2 operations on explicit user approval at invocation time.
Gate tier-3 operations on elevated approval controls and deny execution until satisfied.
Use explicit statuses: ready_to_invoke, blocked_pending_consent, blocked_pending_permission_scope, blocked_policy_denied, tool_unavailable.
